2012-02-01.title: Britain: 2 Libyans Sue Former Counterterrorism Chief
2012-02-01.abstract: Two Libyan men have filed suit against a former official at Britain’s MI6 intelligence service, which they say colluded in operations that delivered them to Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi’s secret police.

2012-02-01.title: Support for Palestinian Authority Erodes as Prices and Taxes Rise
2012-02-01.abstract: Over the past two weeks, Palestinians have taken to the streets in the West Bank to protest the policies of Salam Fayyad, the prime minister of the Palestinian Authority.

2012-02-01.title: How ‘General Jack’ Lost His Swagger
2012-02-01.abstract: Jack Idema was an adventurer, an eccentric, a bounty hunter, a former Special Forces soldier and a swaggering con man. And when he was convicted of the kidnapping and torture of Afghan civilians in Kabul in 2004, he insisted he was working for the U.S. Secretary of Defense. Idema, 55, living in self-imposed exile in Mexico, died recently, reportedly of AIDS, after a life that was equal parts bizarre, colorful and felonious.

2012-02-01.title: As Syria Wobbles Under Pressure, Iran Feels the Weight of an Alliance
2012-02-01.abstract: Some American officials and political analysts say the downfall of Syria’s leader could also undermine Iran as its economy reels under sanctions.

2012-02-01.title: In Spanish Village, Everyone’s a Winner, Almost
2012-02-01.abstract: All but one household in Sodeto, Spain, held at least a piece of a winning ticket in the Christmas lottery’s huge first prize of $950 million, the biggest ever.

2012-02-02.title: A Slight Lowering of the Jolly Roger
2012-02-02.abstract: A recent report on sea piracy was greeted with nearly unanimous acclaim, with headlines proclaiming that commercial cargo ships had faced fewer attacks in 2011 than in the previous year. It was the first decline in five years. So, are the high seas safer? Not necessarily.

2012-02-02.title: Mexican General Is Charged in Killings and Abuses
2012-02-02.abstract: The general and his men are accused of carrying out killings, torture and drugs and arms trafficking in Chihuahua State.

2012-02-02.title: Why a Liberal Arts Education Matters
2012-02-02.abstract: A native of Kolkata, India, writes of attending Harvey Mudd College in California, where she studied physics and math, as well as philosophy and creative writing, an experience that taught her how to think critically and ask questions.

2012-02-02.title: Wislawa Szymborska, Nobel-Winning Polish Poet, Dies at 88
2012-02-02.abstract: Ms. Szymborska was a gentle and reclusive Polish poet who won the 1996 Nobel Prize in Literature.

2012-02-02.title: Syrian Defector: More Guns, Less Gandhi
2012-02-02.abstract: With his slight figure and pensive disposition, Ammar Cheikh Omar seems more like a contemplative philosophy student than a would-be Syrian Rambo. But the 29-year-old defector from the Syrian Army insists it is time for the Syrian opposition to shed its passive philosophizing. It is time, he says, to take up arms. He and other young Syrians are fed up with the passive resistance espoused by their opposition leaders. Gandhi’s ideology, he says ruefully, is no match for a loaded AK-47. Guns must be met with guns.

2012-02-03.title: With Risk, Japanese City Takes On Once Accepted Fact of Life: Its Gangsters
2012-02-03.abstract: Kitakyushu has declared war on the yakuza, just as authorities say any romantic aura that may have enveloped gangsters in the past is falling away in Japanese society.

2012-02-03.title: Wishing Upon an Atom in a Tiny French Village
2012-02-03.abstract: In the village of Fessenheim, an aging nuclear plant stirs both protests and support over the use of nuclear power across France.

2012-02-03.title: For Greek Tax Reformers, Good Ideas Aren’t Enough
2012-02-03.abstract: The Greek government is proving powerless to transform an inefficient public administration that has served as a power base for the same political leaders now being asked to dismantle it.

2012-02-03.title: Britain: Assange Hearing Ends
2012-02-03.abstract: During the last day of a two-day hearing, prosecutors argued that failing to send Julian Assange to Sweden in a sexual misconduct case would “undermine” the extradition system in Europe.

2012-02-03.title: Turkey: Ex-Chief of Military Is Charged
2012-02-03.abstract: Ilker Basbug was charged with leading a leading a terrorist group and masterminding coup plots to overthrow the Islamic-inspired government.

2012-02-04.title: From Success at Putin’s Side to Exposing Corruption
2012-02-04.abstract: Sergei Kolesnikov became a prosperous businessman and part of the crony capitalist web surrounding Vladimir V. Putin. Now he is waging a risky campaign to expose corruption.

2012-02-04.title: South Sudan: Violence at Peace Meeting
2012-02-04.abstract: More than 10 people were killed and a United Nations employee was shot and wounded when clashes broke out at a peace meeting in South Sudan, United Nations officials said Friday.

2012-02-04.title: Obama Got Message Supporting Talks With Taliban, but Maybe Not From Its Leader
2012-02-04.abstract: The unsigned letter purporting to be from Mullah Muhammad Omar was sent last year and also pressed the president to release Taliban prisoners.

2012-02-04.title: Frigid Temperatures Claim Lives of Children in Kabul
2012-02-04.abstract: At least 22 children have died in camps in Kabul in the past month, including 14 at the Nasaji Bagrami camp.

2012-02-04.title: Driven Away by a War, Now Stalked by Winter’s Cold
2012-02-04.abstract: In the past month, at least 22 Afghan children under the age of 5 have frozen to death in refugee camps in Kabul, prompting deep concerns among aid workers.

2012-02-05.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-02-05.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-02-05.title: Sneaking Into Class From China
2012-02-05.abstract: The letters are arriving now from American universities — the dreaded thin envelopes that suggest an application has been denied, and the fat ones that indicate acceptance. In many cases, according to anecdotal evidence and hard-data surveys, the successful Chinese applicants will have cheated their way into college.

2012-02-05.title: ‘The Arab Spring Is Coming to China.’
2012-02-05.abstract: The former Republican presidential nominee, Senator John McCain, told one of China’s most senior diplomats that “the Arab Spring is coming to China,” pointing to the wave of Tibetans setting themselves on fire in China. With the news Sunday of three more Tibetan self-immolations, at least 18 ethnic Tibetans — many of them current or former Buddhist clergy members — have set themselves alight in the past year. The Chinese diplomat, Zhang Zhijun, dismissed the senator’s comments as “more than a fantasy.”

2012-02-05.title: Hello? Pyongyang? Can You Hear Me Now?
2012-02-05.abstract: Orascom, the Egyptian mobile-phone operator with an exclusive contract in North Korea, said last week that it now has more than 1 million subscribers in the Hermit Kingdom. So a slow-motion digital revolution seems to be taking place, if not an actual revolution.

2012-02-05.title: Afghan Suicide Attack Targets Police in Kandahar
2012-02-05.abstract: A man blew up his car in a parking lot in the fourth such attempted or successful bombing in Kandahar in less than a month.

2012-02-06.title: Syrian Unrest After a Failure of Diplomacy
2012-02-06.abstract: With efforts in the United Nations failing, the Syrian government pressed on with its crackdown as some opposition leaders said force was their only option.

2012-02-06.title: When Talk of War Transcends Idle Chatter
2012-02-06.abstract: Few who have spent time with Israel’s decision makers recently have come away believing that the talk of a military assault on Iran is merely a scripted act of public diplomacy.

2012-02-06.title: Solution on Syria Remains Elusive for White House
2012-02-06.abstract: Officials are looking for a way to remove President Bashar al-Assad from power, but to do so the United States may have to move beyond diplomatic efforts.

2012-02-06.title: With Libyan Arms, Mali Fighting Is Revived
2012-02-06.abstract: After fighting for Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi as he struggled to stay in power, Tuareg rebels helped themselves to sophisticated weaponry before returning to Mali.

2012-02-06.title: Abbas Considered to Lead Interim Palestinian Body
2012-02-06.abstract: According to Palestinian reports, an interim unity government would be led, at least at first, by Mahmoud Abbas, the current president of the Palestinian Authority.

2012-02-07.title: Britain: Judge Orders Release on Bail of Militant Accused of Qaeda Role
2012-02-07.abstract: An immigration appeals judge ordered the government on Monday to release on bail a militant Islamic preacher who is considered a leader of Al Qaeda in Europe.

2012-02-07.title: Britain: Quiet Anniversary for a Queen
2012-02-07.abstract: Queen Elizabeth II broke with her custom of performing no public duties on the anniversary of the death of her father, King George VI, in 1952.

2012-02-07.title: Myanmar: Government Panel Approves Opposition Leader’s Run for Parliament
2012-02-07.abstract: An elections panel on Monday affirmed the candidacy of the opposition leader Daw Aung San Suu Kyi for Parliament.

2012-02-07.title: U.S. Sending Commander to Repair Ties With Pakistan
2012-02-07.abstract: Gen. James N. Mattis, the head of the military’s Central Command, will discuss the airstrike that killed 24 Pakistani soldiers and wrecked relations.

2012-02-07.title: With Pisa’s Tower Straighter, Others Vie for Title
2012-02-07.abstract: Repairs have ended the Pisa tower’s status as the most leaning, and a small tower in Germany is the new Guinness titleholder, but rivals have emerged.

2012-02-08.title: Chinese Students Paying U.S. Tuitions — How? And Why?
2012-02-08.abstract: A Chinese freshman at an American public university will typically pay three times what an in-state student pays. And with increasing numbers of Chinese kids at U.S. colleges, even with a booming economy back home, how are they paying more than $50,000 a year? A high school educator from one of Beijing’s leading schools explains the how, the why and the how much.

2012-02-08.title: Myanmar’s Guerrilla Golfers Take Time for a Few Rounds
2012-02-08.abstract: The six-hole golf course in rebel territory in Kachin State may be among the world’s unlikeliest, but it is open, even in wartime.

2012-02-08.title: Radical U.S. Muslims Little Threat, Study Says
2012-02-08.abstract: The study found that arrests of Muslim Americans in plots or violent attacks have dropped sharply since 2009.

2012-02-08.title: Italian Court Affirms Detention of Cruise Ship’s Captain
2012-02-08.abstract: A Florence appeals court rejected both the prosecutors’ request to imprison the Francesco Schettino, who is under house arrest, and the request of his lawyer to have him released.

2012-02-08.title: Elections in Uttar Pradesh
2012-02-08.abstract: India’s biggest state is also its biggest political prize and, with elections this month, its biggest political unknown.

2012-02-09.title: A Cultural Clash Over the Bad Old Days
2012-02-09.abstract: The people of Tijuana, Mexico, are of two minds on the demolition of the infamous La Ocho prison and the future of a city that has been known for its vice.

2012-02-09.title: A Turning Point for Tijuana
2012-02-09.abstract: The people of Tijuana, Mexico, are of two minds on the demolition of the infamous prison La Ocho and the future of a city that was known for its vice.

2012-02-09.title: Tough Words From Iran’s Leaders as Sanctions Cut Deep
2012-02-09.abstract: Beneath the bombast from Iran’s leaders, strains on the economy from the sanctions showed further signs of getting worse, and Iranians appeared worried.

2012-02-09.title: Questions Raised in Afghan Detainee’s Case
2012-02-09.abstract: A military defense team found new evidence that cast doubt on decade-old terrorism charges and offers a case study on how those deemed to be low-level insurgents remain stuck at Guantánamo Bay.

2012-02-09.title: Perfect Timing for ‘The Lady’
2012-02-09.abstract: The timing couldn’t have been better for the Asian release of “The Lady,” a much-anticipated new film about Aung San Suu Kyi. The pro-democracy leader from Myanmar has been freed from house arrest and the government is allowing her to run for Parliament in April elections. The film will be released next month in Singapore and Taiwan, although there is no word on whether it will be shown in mainland China, a close political and economic ally of Myanmar. Not unexpectedly, the film has been banned in Myanmar. When a Hong Kong journalist asked about rumors that the pirated DVD version was already available there, the director Luc Besson responded that that would be “excellent news.”

2012-02-10.title: In Area Embodying Putin Base, Dispirited City Voices Tepid Support
2012-02-10.abstract: A working-class city in Russia embodies support for Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin, though it seems to be based mostly on an absence of options.

2012-02-10.title: Mexico Seizes Record Amount of Methamphetamine
2012-02-10.abstract: The Mexican authorities seized 15 tons of the drug in pure powder form, an amount that could be broken down to 13 million doses worth $4 billion.

2012-02-10.title: 417.6 Million Bites Later ...
2012-02-10.abstract: A 2007 video of a boy chomping on the finger of his brother has been viewed 417.6 million times, making it the most successful noncommercial video in YouTube history.

2012-02-10.title: Demonstrations Whisper of an Arab Spring in Jordan
2012-02-10.abstract: King Abdullah II has long faced critics among liberals in the cities, but recent protests among tribes outside of the urban areas present a new challenge.

2012-02-10.title: China: Self-Immolation Protest
2012-02-10.abstract: Chinese security forces shot and killed two Tibetans on Thursday who had been involved in antigovernment protests, the American broadcaster Radio Free Asia reported.

2012-02-11.title: Like How Your Hair Hangs? Praise the Laws of Physics
2012-02-11.abstract: A head of 100,000 hairs is an intricate system. Elasticity, density, curl and length determine the shape of a ponytail when all those strands are bundled together.

2012-02-11.title: Taking Care of His Adopted Country, One Emergency at a Time
2012-02-11.abstract: Dr. Raed Arafat returned to his post at the Romanian Health Ministry after protesters in the streets supported him.

2012-02-11.title: What to Read: Meditations on a World Divided
2012-02-11.abstract: From the “big book” of the moment in America to the best essays about man, machine, and work, today and tomorrow, Page Two columnist Chrystia Freeland provides your abridged reading list to understanding the great 1 percent/99 percent divide.

2012-02-11.title: Turkey: Intelligence Chief Must Testify
2012-02-11.abstract: Their testimony is part of an investigation into a Kurdish separatist group and is the latest round in a power struggle between the country’s security forces and its intelligence agency.

2012-02-11.title: Hamas Premier Visits Iran in Sign of Strong Relations
2012-02-11.abstract: Amid reports of tensions over Syria, Hamas appeared to be trying to keep Prime Minister Ismail Haniya’s visit low-key, while Iran’s news media played it up.

2012-02-12.title: W. Gunther Plaut, Defined Reform Judaism, Dies at 99
2012-02-12.abstract: Rabbi Plaut’s vast, scholarly and ardently contemporary edition of the Torah helped define Reform Judaism in late-20th-century North America.

2012-02-12.title: Istvan Csurka, Hungarian Playwright and Gadfly, Dies at 77
2012-02-12.abstract: Mr. Csurka, the founder of a far-right political party and a fierce anti-Semite, appealed to older Hungarians with his evocation of the nation’s glory days before World War I.

2012-02-12.title: Risks of Afghan War Shift From Soldiers to Contractors
2012-02-12.abstract: More civilian contractors working for American companies than American soldiers died in Afghanistan last year for the first time, and some survivors are left uncompensated.

2012-02-12.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-02-12.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-02-12.title: The Delicately Poised Chemistry of Diplomacy
2012-02-12.abstract: This will be autumn of the patriarch in China, with Hu Jintao stepping down and presumably passing the leadership of the Communist Party to Xi Jinping, the current vice president. Mr. Xi is due to visit the United States this week, starting with meetings at the White House on Tuesday. He and President Obama will likely discuss the delicately poised chemistry of human rights, even as Beijing continues to practice the alchemy of authoritarianism.

2012-02-13.title: Behind Maldives’ Glamor, a Struggling Democracy
2012-02-13.abstract: For most of the last three decades, the country’s autocratic ruler pursued policies that kept many Maldivians poor while extending a warm welcome to well-heeled foreign tourists. That is changing.

2012-02-13.title: Inside China’s Greatest Mystery
2012-02-13.abstract: China is obsessed with what has happened to the country’s most renowned crime fighter, Wang Lijun. The Chinese blogosphere has registered nearly two million entries about his rumored flirtation with defecting to the United States, his connections to power and his whereabouts.

2012-02-13.title: For Iraqis, Aid to Rebels in Syria Repays a Debt
2012-02-13.abstract: Syrians sent money and weapons to Iraq and accepted its refugees just a few years ago; now Iraqis hope to return the favor.

2012-02-13.title: Guerrilla Faction Leader Is Captured in Peru
2012-02-13.abstract: Florindo Eleuterio Flores Hala, the leader of one of the remaining Shining Path factions, was arrested after a gunfight.

2012-02-13.title: Opposition Voters in Venezuela Pick a Challenger for Chávez
2012-02-13.abstract: Venezuela’s political opposition overcame years of division to choose Henrique Capriles Radonski to take on President Hugo Chávez in what is shaping up to be a potentially tight election campaign.

2012-02-14.title: Bin Laden Link to ‘Demolition Man’?
2012-02-14.abstract: It was strange enough that the alleged bomb maker — a senior member of a violent terror network in Southeast Asia — nearly blew himself up right before his handiwork killed 202 people in Bali in 2002. Odder still that he was arrested nearly a decade later, 4,000 miles away, in the very same city where Osama bin Laden had sought refuge. Umar Patek’s trial on terror-related charges began Monday in Jakarta.

2012-02-14.title: Turkmenistan’s President Re-elected With 97% of Vote
2012-02-14.abstract: The incumbent, Gurbanguly Berdymukhammedov, trounced seven token candidates.

2012-02-14.title: American Recounts Beating by Chinese Agents Suspicious of Social Media
2012-02-14.abstract: A naturalized citizen active on the Internet in support of human rights said officers had abducted and interrogated him during a visit.

2012-02-14.title: French Candidate Assails Plan for Greece
2012-02-14.abstract: The Socialist candidate for the French presidency, François Hollande, criticized European policy on Greece, saying that mandatory austerity measures were too severe.

2012-02-14.title: A Nuclear Plant, and a Dream, Fizzles
2012-02-14.abstract: Filipino scientists long dreamed of opening the first nuclear plant in Southeast Asia, but they have had to settle for an exhibit of what might have been.

2012-02-15.title: Nuclear Peekaboo by North Korea, Part Deux?
2012-02-15.abstract: Every diplomat from Foggy Bottom to Smoggy Beijing is anxious to see what Kim Jong-un’s foreign policies are going to look like. The new North Korean leader does possess, after all, a handful of nuclear weapons. A glimpse into his inclinations could come next week, when U.S. and North Korean envoys meet for direct talks in Beijing.

2012-02-15.title: From Overseas Visitors, a Growing Demand to Study the Holocaust
2012-02-15.abstract: At Yad Vashem, Israel’s memorial and museum to the Holocaust, educators have found that lessons about individuals help illustrate the Holocaust’s universal lessons to non-Jewish visitors.

2012-02-15.title: Amid Image of Ire Toward U.S. Bases, Okinawans’ True Views Vary
2012-02-15.abstract: The island of Okinawa in Japan can seem united in its resistance to the American military bases it hosts, but the depth of that opposition varies across the political spectrum.

2012-02-15.title: Italy Appeal Seeks Retrial of American in ’07 Killing
2012-02-15.abstract: Italian prosecutors filed an appeal to the country’s Supreme Court four months after Amanda Knox’s 2009 murder conviction was overturned.

2012-02-15.title: Iran Leader to Announce Atomic Steps
2012-02-15.abstract: President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad is likely to say that a new uranium enrichment plant, a source of Western concern, is now completely functional, an Iranian news agency said.

2012-02-16.title: U.S. Pushes to Cut Emissions of Some Pollutants That Hasten Climate Change
2012-02-16.abstract: A small group of countries led by the United States is starting a program to address short-lived pollutants like soot, methane and hydrofluorocarbons that account for 30 to 40 percent of global warming.

2012-02-16.title: The Decline and Fall of Greece
2012-02-16.abstract: “I believe that by the end of 2012, you will see a different Greece, a different country, with real poverty,” a Greek banker told Russell Shorto in an article for the magazine. Already, the Chinese have largely taken over Greece’s main port, Qatar is investing billions buying infrastructure and rich Europeans are buying islands to make retirement villages.

2012-02-16.title: Sarkozy Will Run for Second Term as French President
2012-02-16.abstract: With a restive party pressing him to run in earnest for re-election, President Nicolas Sarkozy of France announced his candidacy. His main challenger, François Hollande, is leading in polls.

2012-02-16.title: Cuba Unleashes the Pent-Up Energy of Real Estate Dreams
2012-02-16.abstract: Cubans are injecting money into real estate, spurred by government measures to stimulate construction and a new law that allows property trades for the first time in 50 years.

2012-02-16.title: Doubting Greeks’ Resolve, Euro Zone May Hold Back Full Bailout
2012-02-16.abstract: European finance ministers discussed a plan on Wednesday under which they might pay only about $40 billion to help assure that Greece could meet a $19 billion bond payment in late March.

2012-02-17.title: Iraqi Official Was Behind 150 Attacks, Judges Say
2012-02-17.abstract: A judicial panel says death squads commanded by Vice President Tariq al-Hashimi carried out 150 attacks over six years against religious pilgrims, security officers and political foes.

2012-02-17.title: Egyptian Party Threatens to Review Treaty With Israel
2012-02-17.abstract: The Muslim Brotherhood said it would review the 1979 peace treaty with Israel if the United States cuts off Egypt’s aid over a crackdown on nonprofit groups.

2012-02-17.title: For Some in Mexico, Trash Is a Treasure Worth Defending
2012-02-17.abstract: Efforts by Mexico City officials to recycle, burn and compost more of the city’s garbage stands in sharp relief to the needs of 1,500 trash pickers who depend on the refuse.

2012-02-17.title: German President Could Lose His Immunity
2012-02-17.abstract: Prosecutors requested that Parliament strip President Christian Wulff of his immunity, saying they had “factual indications” of his inappropriate connections to executives.

2012-02-17.title: What if the Renminbi Is Not the Problem?
2012-02-17.abstract: The renminbi is up 12 percent since June 2010 on an inflation-adjusted basis and 40 percent since 2005, reports David Leonhardt. Increasingly, executives and economists are suggesting that the West should move on to other issues with China.

2012-02-18.title: The Rise of the Asians at New York Fashion Week
2012-02-18.abstract: Suzy Menkes, the IHT’s fashion editor, tells Charlie Rose that Asian-American designers were “the stars” of New York Fashion Week. Now London begins.

2012-02-18.title: Momentum Builds for Syria Action Ahead of Tunis Conference
2012-02-18.abstract: Backers of an Arab League peace plan said Friday that they were seeking new ways to aid opponents of President Bashar al-Assad and to ensure next week’s conference puts additional pressure on him.

2012-02-18.title: Police Raid Moscow Bank Owned by Putin Critic
2012-02-18.abstract: Federal agents conducted a search of the National Reserve Bank owned by Aleksandr Y. Lebedev, a billionaire tycoon who owns a newspaper critical of the Kremlin.

2012-02-18.title: Honduras: U.N. Calls for Inquiry Into Fire at Prison
2012-02-18.abstract: The United Nations human rights office called Friday for an independent investigation into a fire that killed more than 350 inmates at a prison in Comayagua on Tuesday.

2012-02-18.title: China: A Ranking Monk’s Protest Suicide
2012-02-18.abstract: A Tibetan monk died after setting fire to himself Friday, another sign of the trouble in China’s western border region.

2012-02-19.title: South Africans Suffer as Graft Saps Provinces
2012-02-19.abstract: Officials in Limpopo Province overspent their budget by about $250 million, much of it on questionable or fraudulent payments and contracts with businesses linked to the province’s politicians.

2012-02-19.title: Did ESPN Cross the Foul Line Into Racism?
2012-02-19.abstract: There has been an angry and growing reaction to the phrase “chink in the armor’’ that appeared on ESPN — twice — in connection with New York Knicks guard Jeremy Lin, the Taiwanese-American sensation.

2012-02-19.title: India Allows Request to Charge U.S. Citizen in 2008 Attacks
2012-02-19.abstract: The decision is the first step in seeking an extradition for David Coleman Headley in connection with the terrorist attacks in Mumbai.

2012-02-19.title: Mumbai Voters Skeptical of Promises to Revive City
2012-02-19.abstract: Maharashtra State’s chief minister couldn’t convince Mumbai voters that his party would rejuvenate the city.

2012-02-19.title: Using Cinema to ‘Softly’ Tackle Taboos in Turkey
2012-02-19.abstract: Webcasts from the !f Istanbul International Independent Film Festival are seeking common ground in Turkey and beyond.

2012-02-20.title: For Woman in Sudan, No Escape From Misery
2012-02-20.abstract: Having seen her first husband and two of her children die, being a southerner in an inhospitable northern land is the latest hardship Mary Nyekueh Ley says she must endure.

2012-02-20.title: Beijing’s Man in Hong Kong Comes Under Fire
2012-02-20.abstract: Relations between Hong Kongers and mainland Chinese have deteriorated badly in recent weeks, and the friction has found its way into the race for Hong Kong’s next chief executive. Henry Tang, thought by many to be Beijing’s preferred candidate, is under fire for illegal renovations to a luxury home owned by his wife.

2012-02-20.title: Pressed by Unrest and Money Woes, Egypt Accepts I.M.F. Loan
2012-02-20.abstract: The country’s transitional government said it would accept a deal largely like the one the country’s military rulers had turned down last spring.

2012-02-20.title: Iran Raid Seen as a Huge Task for Israeli Jets
2012-02-20.abstract: An attack would take at least 100 planes, American defense officials and military analysts said, and take far more time than earlier strikes on nuclear sites in Syria and Iraq.

2012-02-20.title: Taliban Gaining More Resources From Kidnapping
2012-02-20.abstract: Ransom kidnapping has long been a problem in Pakistan, but the increased involvement by the Taliban and their allies adds a new and disturbing element.

2012-02-21.title: Names of the Dead
2012-02-21.abstract: The Department of Defense has identified 1,883 American service members who have died as a part of the Afghan war and related operations. It confirmed the deaths of the following Americans recently.

2012-02-21.title: For Russia, a New Kind of Presidential Candidate: a Billionaire
2012-02-21.abstract: Mikhail D. Prokhorov, a fresh face in Russian politics, must overcome suspicions about the rich and a powerful opponent, Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin.

2012-02-21.title: Yemen’s President Cedes Authority With Election, but Hopes to Retain Influence
2012-02-21.abstract: Ali Abdullah Saleh signaled his hope to retain some degree of influence on his country’s affairs a day before an election in which his vice president is the only candidate on the ballot.

2012-02-21.title: Europe Agrees on New Bailout to Help Greece Avoid Default
2012-02-21.abstract: After more than 12 hours of talks, euro zone finance ministers approved a new bailout of 130 billion euros, or $172 billion, under which private investors in Greek debt will take steep losses to help stave off a default.

2012-02-21.title: Ireland Makes Pitch to Official From China
2012-02-21.abstract: Following an official visit to Ireland by Chinese Vice President Xi Jinping, Irish government officials said their country has positioned itself to become China’s springboard into Europe.

2012-02-22.title: Saying Goodbye to Shadid
2012-02-22.abstract: A memorial service for Anthony Shadid was held at the American University of Beirut.

2012-02-22.title: Israel Watches Syria, Hopefully, but Warily
2012-02-22.abstract: The fall of President Bashar al-Assad of Syria would be welcome in Jerusalem, but the prospect of chaos across the border worries officials.

2012-02-22.title: Guards Implicated in Mexico Prison’s Deadly Gang Attack
2012-02-22.abstract: Officials in the state of Nuevo León said the jailbreak and the massacre had been carried out by the Zetas, a gang of drug enforcers who appeared to have prison authorities under control.

2012-02-22.title: Saying Goodbye to Anthony Shadid
2012-02-22.abstract: Family and friends gathered to mourn the death of Anthony Shadid, a journalist for The New York Times and author, who died in Syria last week.

2012-02-22.title: Chávez Says His Cancer Shows Sign of Return
2012-02-22.abstract: President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela said that doctors had found a new lesion that could indicate a return of his cancer, and that he would undergo an operation.

2012-02-23.title: Pentagon Says U.S. Citizens With Terrorism Ties Can Be Targeted in Strikes
2012-02-23.abstract: The Obama administration’s top Pentagon lawyer on Wednesday said that American citizens who join Al Qaeda can be targeted for killing and that courts should have no role in reviewing executive branch decisions about whether someone has met such criteria.

2012-02-23.title: Two Journalists Shared Desire to Bear Witness in World’s Hot Spots
2012-02-23.abstract: Marie Colvin, 56, an American correspondent, covered war-torn regions for The Sunday Times of London, and Rémi Ochlik, 28, a French freelancer, was a rising star among photojournalists.

2012-02-23.title: Mainland Chinese Flock to Hong Kong to Give Birth
2012-02-23.abstract: Parents go to Hong Kong for better medical care and lifelong residency benefits for their children, but locals are outraged over being shut out of maternity wards.

2012-02-23.title: Israeli Court Invalidates a Military Exemption
2012-02-23.abstract: The 6-to-3 decision, which overturns the exemption of ultra-Orthodox Jews engaged in religious studies from military service, adds a new urgency to the government’s negotiations with religious parties.

2012-02-23.title: Shelled, Blinded and Stranded
2012-02-23.abstract: Excerpts from Marie Colvin’s war reporting in The Sunday Times of London.

2012-02-24.title: In New Protests, Echoes of an Uprising That Shook Sudan
2012-02-24.abstract: A police raid last weekend stirred memories of Sudan’s 1964 October Revolution, when students stood up to the military regime and helped restore democracy.

2012-02-24.title: Pierre Juneau, Champion of Canada’s Pop Music Industry, Dies at 89
2012-02-24.abstract: Mr. Juneau helped create Canada’s pop music industry when, as a member of the Trudeau government in 1970, he required radio stations to broadcast more Canadian music.

2012-02-24.title: Killing of 2 Americans Came as U.S. Reviewed Afghan Security
2012-02-24.abstract: The killing of two American service members by a man in an Afghan Army uniform occurred as the United States military was reviewing procedures used to vet Afghan security forces.

2012-02-24.title: Resolute Putin Faces a Russia That’s Changed
2012-02-24.abstract: Prime Minister Vladimir V. Putin, likely to be elected president next month, will face a Russian society that has greatly changed under his watch, while he has remained essentially the same.

2012-02-24.title: After a Year, Deep Divisions Hobble Syria’s Opposition
2012-02-24.abstract: Nearly a year into Syria’s uprising, the opposition to President Bashar al-Assad is a fractious collection of divided political groups, longtime exiles, grass-roots organizers and militants.

2012-02-25.title: Stages in Developing a Nuclear Nation
2012-02-25.abstract: New details on Iran’s nuclear program.

2012-02-25.title: An Academic Turns Grief Into a Crime-Fighting Tool
2012-02-25.abstract: After her son was killed by the police, Julieta Castellanos, who leads Honduras’s largest university, has become an unlikely hero by demanding that the nation finally address its growing crime rate.

2012-02-25.title: Egypt Court to Issue Verdict in Mubarak’s Trial in June
2012-02-25.abstract: The timing is expected to coincide with a presidential election, after which the military council has pledged to cede power.

2012-02-25.title: Yemen Gets New Leader as Struggle Ends Calmly
2012-02-25.abstract: After a one-candidate election, Vice President Abdu Rabbu Mansour Hadi will be sworn in as president on Saturday morning, ending Ali Abdullah Saleh’s three decades of rule.

2012-02-25.title: U.S.-Russian Trade Ties Face Some Political Snags
2012-02-25.abstract: Despite tense relations, the Obama administration has begun an aggressive push to end cold-war-era trade restrictions, in a move to avoid violations from the World Trade Organization and reset the country’s relationship with the Kremlin.

2012-02-26.title: Haitian Premier Says Loss of Support Led Him to Quit
2012-02-26.abstract: Prime Minister Garry Conille of Haiti, who has clashed with the president, said he knew he had to resign after no one showed up at a cabinet meeting he had called.

2012-02-26.title: New From Apple! The iStove!
2012-02-26.abstract: Such is the power of the Apple name and brand in China that an enterprising company there has been making one-burner gas stoves carrying the once-bitten Apple logo and a label that says “iphone.” Stove, cellphone, whatever — it’s the latest example of the Apple fakery industry in China.

2012-02-26.title: New From Apple! The iStove!
2012-02-26.abstract: Such is the power of the Apple name and brand in China that an enterprising company there has been making one-burner gas stoves carrying the once-bitten Apple logo and a label that says “iphone.” Stove, cellphone, whatever — it’s the latest example of the Apple fakery industry in China.

2012-02-26.title: Sorry Seems to Be the Hardest Word
2012-02-26.abstract: The formal apology — it’s not something that any American president takes lightly or offers easily, although most recent presidents have offered multiple apologies. President Obama’s letter of apology to President Hamid Karzai over the burning of some Korans by U.S. troops in Afghanistan has angered his political opponents and detractors.

2012-02-26.title: Sorry Seems to Be the Hardest Word
2012-02-26.abstract: The formal apology — it’s not something that any American president takes lightly or offers easily, although most recent presidents have offered multiple apologies. President Obama’s letter of apology to President Hamid Karzai over the burning of some Korans by U.S. troops in Afghanistan has angered his political opponents and detractors.

2012-02-27.title: U.S. Teaming With New Yemen Government on Strategy to Combat Al Qaeda
2012-02-27.abstract: The Obama administration’s potentially risky plan would help Yemen overhaul its military to take on a Qaeda franchise that has exploited political turmoil there.

2012-02-27.title: A Taste of Hope Sends Refugees Back to Darfur
2012-02-27.abstract: More than 100,000 people have left the sprawling camps where they took refuge for nearly a decade and headed home to their villages in Darfur over the past year.

2012-02-27.title: Afghan Uproar Casts Shadows on U.S Pullout
2012-02-27.abstract: Administration officials described growing concern about a drawdown by the United States that hinges on the close mentoring and training of Afghan army and police forces.

2012-02-27.title: Europeans Agree to Review Size of Firewall Fund
2012-02-27.abstract: A meeting of G-20 ministers wins a commitment to review the size of its planned stabilization fund to ward off future crises.

2012-02-27.title: In North Korea, Same As the Old Boss
2012-02-27.abstract: Diplomats and analysts said over the weekend that they detected no signals that the new North Korean regime of Kim Jong-un will be markedly different from that of his father. In short, he’s unlikely to give up the North’s nuclear program. On Monday, the North denounced war games by the United States and South Korea as “unpardonable war hysteria kicked up by the hooligans to desecrate our mourning period’’ over Kim Jong-il. Rendezvous can’t wait to see what the propaganda-spinners in Pyongyang will have to say about the actor Sacha Baron Cohen dumping the “ashes’’ of their late Generalissimo onto the TV presenter Ryan Seacrest during the red-carpet walk-in at the Academy Awards.

2012-02-28.title: Protesters Set New Goal: Fixing Yemen’s Military
2012-02-28.abstract: After Ali Abdullah Saleh’s ouster, Yemen’s protesters are not yet content and now want to overhaul a military still largely controlled by Mr. Saleh’s relatives.

2012-02-28.title: Germany: Nazi Hunter Chosen as Presidential Candidate
2012-02-28.abstract: Beate Klarsfeld, who spent decades fighting fascism and rooting out former Nazis with her husband, will run for president against a former East German rights activist.

2012-02-28.title: President of Ecuador to Pardon Four in Libel Case
2012-02-28.abstract: President Rafael Correa said that he would forgive $42 million in fines levied against a newspaper, El Universo, and the four men, who had been sentenced to three years in prison.

2012-02-28.title: ‘Yunus Was Right,’ SKS Microfinance Founder Says
2012-02-28.abstract: Controversial industry executive says his chief critic was right about difficulties he would face making microfinance turn a profit.

2012-02-28.title: Iraq’s Prime Minister Gains More Power After Political Crisis
2012-02-28.abstract: Worries remain that Iraq is sliding toward one-man, one-party rule under Nuri Kamal al-Maliki amid a sectarian divide.

2012-02-29.title: U.N. Staff Leaves an Afghan Office
2012-02-29.abstract: International staff members were removed from an office in Kunduz, Afghanistan, this week after a riot on Saturday.

2012-02-29.title: French Council Strikes Down Bill on Armenian Genocide Denial
2012-02-29.abstract: Turkey applauded the move, but the president of France vowed to try again.

2012-02-29.title: 2-Year-Old’s Death Draws Attention to Ireland’s ‘Ghost’ Housing Developments
2012-02-29.abstract: The nation is torn over what to do with thousands of unfinished housing developments, known as ghost estates, that are a legacy of boom times and lie incomplete and underused.

2012-02-29.title: Syria’s Sectarian Fears Keep Region on Edge
2012-02-29.abstract: The insurrection is increasingly dangerous and unpredictable because it is aggravating tensions beyond Syria’s borders.

2012-02-29.title: Party Leader Leaves India for Health Care
2012-02-29.abstract: The departure of Sonia Gandhi, leader of the governing party, raises new questions about the state of her health.
